Confidentiality.entity_description: Confidentiality is a legal principle that ensures that certain information is kept private and not disclosed to unauthorized parties. It is often a critical aspect of agreements to protect sensitive information.

Privilege.entity_description: Privilege refers to a legal right that allows a party to withhold information from disclosure in legal proceedings. It is often associated with attorney-client communications and other confidential relationships.
